senses.definition: a plural form referring to half-moon shapes or crescent forms
examples: The sky was lined with semilunes, faintly glowing at dusk.
They arranged semilunes in a decorative pattern on the vase.

senses.definition: partly shiny or luminous; having a semi-lustrous quality
examples: The fabric has a semilustrous sheen under the light.
She wore semilustrous accessories that caught the eye without being flashy.

senses.definition: partial dislocation; a partial misalignment of a joint
examples: The doctor described the injury as a semiluxation of the finger.
Rehabilitation followed after the semiluxation was treated.

senses.definition: a quality or level of luxury that is less than full luxury; moderate luxury
examples: The hotel offered semiluxury accommodations at a lower price.
They chose semiluxury cars for the trip.

senses.definition: partly lyrical; possessing some lyrical quality
examples: The poem has semilyric passages that express emotion vividly.
Her semilyric voice gave the performance a soft, musical touch.

senses.definition: partly comic or humorous in a manner that resembles a parody or satire
examples: The scene had semilyrical humor that made the audience smile.
Her semilyrical drawing exaggerated features for effect.

senses.definition: in a semilyrical manner; with partial lyric or humorous parodic tone
examples: The description semilyrically praised the hero while poking fun at his flaws.
She spoke semilyrically, blending poetry with humor.

senses.definition: a machine that is partly mechanical; not fully automated
examples: The workshop uses a semimachine setup for efficiency while still requiring manual input.
They integrated a semimachine system in the production line.

senses.definition: partially manufactured or produced; not fully completed
examples: The semimade components require finishing before assembly.
They sold semimade furniture with customizable options.

senses.definition: a person who is semi-mad or very eccentric; not fully insane
examples: In the story, he plays the role of a semimadman who challenges norms.
The character is depicted as a semimadman with odd quirks.

senses.definition: partly magical; possessing some magical qualities
examples: The forest held semimagical lights that guided travelers.
She told semimagical stories that felt real to the children.

senses.definition: in a semimagical way; with partial magical impression
examples: The tale unfolds semimagically, blurring lines between reality and fantasy.
They semimagically claimed the artifact could grant a wish.

senses.definition: a magnet with partially weaker magnetic strength or partial magnetic properties
examples: The device uses a semimagnet to generate a weak field.
Researchers tested semimagnet materials for redundancy.

senses.definition: partly magnetic or showing magnetic properties
examples: The semimagnetical sample exhibited a faint pull toward iron.
They studied semimagnetical materials for sensors.

senses.definition: in a semimagnetical way; with partial magnetic properties
examples: The detector responded semimagnetically to the sample.
The material behaved semimagnetically under the test field.

senses.definition: the larger axis of an ellipse or orbital path; farthest from the center
examples: The planet has a semimajor axis of 1.5 AU.
The ellipse’s semimajor length determines its overall size.

senses.definition: partly malicious or with a hint of cruel intent
examples: The note carried semimalicious taunts.
Her semimalicious prank caused a lot of trouble.

senses.definition: in a semimalicious way; with partly malicious intent
examples: He smiled semimaliciously, enjoying the confusion he caused.
The note was semimaliciously crafted to mislead.

senses.definition: the quality of being semimalicious; partial malice or slyness
examples: The semimaliciousness of the joke masked its harmful edge.
Her semimaliciousness made others wary.

senses.definition: partly malignant or harmful in nature
examples: The tumor was described as semimalignant by the clinician.
Semimalignant effects were noted in the early studies.

senses.definition: in a semimalignant manner; with partial malice or aggressiveness
examples: He spoke semimalignantly, challenging every assumption.
The advertising campaign semimalignantly pushed fear.

senses.definition: partly managerial in function or role; combining management duties with other tasks
examples: She held a semimanagerial position, handling both staff and projects.
The team adopted semimanagerial practices to stay flexible.

senses.definition: in a semimanagerial way; with both management and other duties
examples: They organized the project semimanagerially to balance oversight with hands-on work.
The department operates semimanagerially during transitions.

senses.definition: partly manneristic; following stylistic manners or affected style partially
examples: The painting has semimanneristic features, echoing late Renaissance taste.
Her semimanneristic dialogue imitates formal mannerisms.

senses.definition: a stage of production where goods are partly finished; not yet fully manufactured
examples: These semimanufactures are awaiting final processing.
The factory exports semimanufacture items for completion elsewhere.
